paper.title: Divergent evolution of hepatocellular carcinoma genomes in chimpanzees and humans

entities.name: Divergent evolution of hepatocellular carcinoma genomes in chimpanzees and humans

evidence_entities.text: Chimpanzee HCCs exhibited histological and immunohistochemical features similar to human tumors but displayed sharply divergent genomic landscapes.
Non-synonymous TSC2 mutations occurred in 80% of chimpanzees, versus ~7% in human HCC, suggesting a species-specific oncogenic pathway linked to the scirrhous subtype.
TSC2 expression in the chimpanzee samples was mainly cytosolic with high immunostaining in four of the samples, including the metastatic lymph node tissue.
All chimpanzee HCC tissues (100%) exhibited strong positive staining for VIM, with high signal intensity.
On average per sample, 5632 mutations were found in chimpanzee samples, while 275 mutations were found in TCGA+ samples and 96 mutations were found in ICGC samples.
